name: AI Red Teaming
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure AI Foundry
services.purpose: Red team orchestration hub — attack scenario management, evaluation pipelines, model endpoint configuration, and safety assessment workflows

prod.note: Automated evaluation pipelines, scheduled red team runs

enterprise.note: Multi-project, dedicated compute for continuous red teaming

services.name: Azure AI Content Safety
services.purpose: Automated safety scoring — evaluates AI responses for hate, violence, self-harm, sexual content, jailbreak detection, and prompt injection

services.name: Azure OpenAI
services.purpose: Dual role — (1) target model under test (GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini), (2) adversarial prompt generator for automated attack synthesis

dev.note: ~100K tokens/day, attack prompt generation + target evaluation

enterprise.note: Provisioned throughput for continuous red teaming across models

services.name: Azure Functions
services.purpose: Red team campaign orchestration — attack scenario execution, response collection, safety evaluation dispatch, report generation

enterprise.note: Always-warm, VNET isolation, parallel attack execution

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Red team findings database — attack scenarios, model responses, safety scores, vulnerability patterns, remediation tracking

services.name: Blob Storage
services.purpose: Red team artifacts — attack prompts, model responses, evaluation reports, evidence screenshots, campaign exports

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Target model API keys, Foundry credentials, Content Safety keys, red team service principal secrets

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Red team pipeline metrics — attack success rates, safety score distributions, model vulnerability trends, campaign coverage

optimization_tips: Use GPT-4o-mini as the adversarial prompt generator — it produces creative attack prompts at 1/10th the cost of GPT-4o, which should be reserved as the target model
Batch red team evaluations during off-peak hours — Content Safety API has lower latency and Functions cost less on Consumption plans during low-traffic periods
Cache known-safe responses by prompt fingerprint — skip Content Safety evaluation for prompts that have been evaluated within the last 24 hours with identical model versions
Use Azure AI Foundry's built-in evaluation metrics before custom evaluations — saves development time and provides standardized safety benchmarks
Implement progressive attack complexity — start with simple prompt injections (low token cost) and only escalate to complex multi-turn attacks for models that pass initial screening
Share Cosmos DB and Blob Storage across multiple target models — red team infrastructure costs are fixed, so test more models to amortize per-model cost
